Title: Alune
Age: CurrentAt least 26 years old Timeline BornEarlier than 971 AN Edit

children.title: Early life
children.textContent:
Alune and Aphelios are twins born to the Lunari during a lunar convergence, when the moon of the spirit realm eclipsed the physical moon, resulting in them being hailed as children with an important destiny. Aphelios was physically gifted like the moon of stone, while Alune was magically gifted by the spirit moon. They both grew up as loyal believers in their faith, one of mystery, discovery and reflection, and embraced the night not only due to their belief, but as their only shield against the Solari, who considered Lunari heretics and sought to destroy them. As a result, the Lunari hid away until it was thought they no longer existed.

Alune's twin Aphelios was burdened by the weight of expectations. He trained with his moonstone blades until he bled, ready to protect his fellow Lunari. He bonded only with his sister, preferring her company to others'. Alune herself trained as a seer, revealing hidden paths and truths by moonlight. As she grew in power, however, the tasks she undertook required her to leave their home.

children.title: The Temple Opens
children.textContent:
With his sister being gone more often, Aphelios grew desperate for purpose. He decided to perform a ceremony which would reveal one's path. In doing so, he discovered a pool where poisonous noctum flowers grew. The blooms could be distilled into a liquid that would open him to the night's power - however, drinking it caused Aphelios to feel so much pain that he became numb to everything else.

One day, the Lunari temple, the Marus Omegnum, began to come into phase to emerge from the Spirit Realm for the first time in centuries. Every time the temple opened, a person gifted in magic was chosen to occupy it. Alune was chosen to be the occupant. Lunari from around the mountain to watch, and Aphelios, who was not known for asking for things, asked to attend.

But as the halls of the temple entered the physical realm, the Solari discovered the ceremony. They attacked the Lunari, defeating Aphelios and causing him to reach for the noctum flowers and eat one. Alune rushed deeper into the temple and eventually reached its heart. There, her magical abilities were amplified. Through the poison in Aphelios' blood Alune could give him power and give him new moonstone weapons.

Alune drew herself and the temple back into the spirit realm to keep it safe from the Solari. Inside, she could send power anywhere, as long as it had a focus, like the poison in Aphelios' blood from eating the noctum flower. With this newfound power, the two of them vowed to protect the Lunari at all costs.

children.title: You Are the Weapon
children.textContent: Aphelios, along with Alune looking through his senses, was in a cave. Preparing for an attack, Aphelios drank poison from the noctum flowers in order to make sure that Alune could better connect to him through the poison in his body. After preparing, Aphelios scaled Mount Targon with the help of his weapons. He reached his destination, an area on the side of the mountain. This area contained several Solari people, who he quickly killed. On the ground, he found a scimitar. With this discovery, he and Alune realized that its owner was running. Aphelios picked it up and walked into the night, intent on finding her.

mainContent.title: Appearence
mainContent.textContent: Alune is a slim, pale-skinned woman with deep purple eyes and long white hair. She wears elegant, flowing robes with a distinct palette of black, purple, and turquoise. A silver diadem ornaments her head and a silver necklace with the Lunari symbol adorns her chest. Alune wears ceremonial paints in the form of a large, white circle on the center of her face to compliment the crescent moon marking of her brother's face.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Alune is a very devoted and mysterious individual but she is also extremely protective and caring for her twin brother, Aphelios. She is often faithful to her goddess whom she refers to as 6 Mother Moon, she always tries to balance her duty as a seer of the moon and her attention and care for her brother, who counts on her guidance.

children.title: Aphelios
children.textContent: Alune and her twin brother Aphelios have had a deep connection to each other since birth, each relying on the other. Alune refers to him as Phel. They have a strong attachment to opposites: light and darkness, physical and spirit, life and death, and so balance each other out.

children.title: Diana
children.textContent: Aphelios and Alune are currently searching for the Aspect of the Moon, Diana.

children.title: The Silver Sister
children.textContent: 6 The Silver Sister is the celestial of the Moon, it is likely that she is the goddess of the Lunari which Alune refers to as Mother Moon.

children.title: Yuumi
children.textContent: Alune is friends with Yuumi and Book of Thresholds, who visit her periodically in the Spirit Realm.

children.title: Soraka
children.textContent: Soraka knows the pain of the Lunari twins, but tells them not to feel it. Alune considers this denying her and her brother their existence.

children.title: Kayle and  Morgana
children.textContent: Alune views Kayle and Morgana as her and Aphelios' predecessors, as they are also twins that represent the dark and the light.

children.title: Leona
children.textContent: As the champion of the Solari, Alune sees Leona as the Lunari's ultimate enemy. However, like most of the Lunari, she is not necessarily hostile towards Leona or the Solari.

children.title: Skins
children.textContent: Alune appears in the background in the splash arts of Aphelios skins.
